routes.name: Yoshida Trail
routes.description: The north-side trail, starting from Fuji Subaru Line 5th Station. About 60% of all climbers pick this one — hut density, bus access from Tokyo, and well-marked paths make it the obvious choice for first-timers.

routes.name: Subashiri Trail
routes.description: Starts lower than Yoshida but offers a beautiful forested approach before merging with Yoshida at the 8th station for sunrise. A favorite of repeat climbers.

routes.name: Fujinomiya Trail
routes.description: The shortest route, thanks to the highest starting altitude. Best for travelers coming from Kyoto or Osaka. Wildflower meadows in midsummer are spectacular.

routes.name: Gotemba Trail
routes.description: The longest and lowest-starting trail, with few huts and raw volcanic scenery. Only for experienced hikers who can handle altitude gain and self-sufficiency.
